html:
Exclusive to:

This content is available only after activating the Lunar Rift

EmptyFull

The Spark Ark is an Item in Don't Starve Together, introduced in From Beyond. It resembles the head of an Ancient Sentrypede.

The first Spark Ark is given to the player by the Grainy Transmission after three lunar-mutated bosses are defeated:

  • Crystal Deerclops
  • Armored Bearger
  • Possessed Varg

Subsequent Spark Arks can be acquired by defeating only one of the bosses mentioned above; however, players must still cycle through the mutations. To receive a Spark Ark from a boss, it must not be a part of the current cycle; the cycle must be completed by having defeated both of the other ones. It is possible to earn a reward for defeating the same boss twice in a row if said boss was the final one of the three that was killed, as the progress is completely reset for each cycle.

For example, if the first Spark Ark is received after the defeat of Crystal Deerclops, the next Spark Ark can be obtained by defeating any one of the three bosses. If Crystal Deerclops is the first boss in the cycle thereafter, the player is rewarded with a Spark Ark. Afterward, one cannot be obtained from Crystal Deerclops again until after both Armored Bearger and Possessed Varg are defeated to conclude the cycle.

To be used in crafting, the Spark Ark must be filled with energy at the activated Ancient Archive. When placed on the floor, the Ark absorbs the Security Pulse from the Ancient Guard Post.

The full Spark Ark can be used to craft the Polar Bearger Bin, Ice Crystaleyezer, and Howlitzer at the Brightsmithy.

Contents

  • 1 Crafting
  • 2 Usage
  • 3 Trivia
  • 4 Gallery

Crafting

This content is available only after defeating Celestial Scion

The Spark Ark cannot be crafted until Celestial Scion is defeated, after which it can no longer be dropped by the Grainy Transmission. Instead, it can be crafted at the Notional Fabricator using 1 Nucleation Fluid, 2 Thulecite and 1 Restrained Static.

Usage

Trivia

  • The Spark Ark was introduced in the Host of Horrors update.
  • On initial beta release, prior to Update 576123, it was only possible to acquire extra Spark Arks after defeating all three lunar-mutated bosses again.

This content is available only after activating the Lunar Rift
EmptyFull
“Interesting. It's empty.”
–Wilson, when examining an empty Spark Ark.
“It's empty. So what?”
–Willow, when examining an empty Spark Ark.
“Is too small for Wolfgang to hide in. Not that Wolfgang need to hide.”
–Wolfgang, when examining an empty Spark Ark.
“I feel the same way.”
–Wendy, when examining an empty Spark Ark.
“DEAD INSIDE. NICE”
–WX-78, when examining an empty Spark Ark.
“I have not come across this in any book.”
–Wickerbottom, when examining an empty Spark Ark.
“Somebody better get to unemptying this trap.”
–Woodie, when examining an empty Spark Ark.
“It's empty. Someone do something.”
–Maxwell, when examining an empty Spark Ark.
“'Tis a trap!”
–Wigfrid, when examining an empty Spark Ark.
“We wonder what goes in there.”
–Webber, when examining an empty Spark Ark.
“This reminds me of my little oven back home.”
–Warly, when examining an empty Spark Ark.
“Big nothing”
–Wormwood, when examining an empty Spark Ark.
“Well, there's a whole lotta nothin' inside.”
–Winona, when examining an empty Spark Ark.
“It's orbless, more or less.”
–Wortox, when examining an empty Spark Ark.
“Glorp! Empty!”
–Wurt, when examining an empty Spark Ark.
“It's empty, Woby. Let's find something to put in there.”
–Walter, when examining an empty Spark Ark.
“It's empty.”
–Wanda, when examining an empty Spark Ark.
“Aren't you the cutest little ball of pure energy?”
–Wilson, when examining a full Spark Ark.
“Great, it glows. But does it burn?”
–Willow, when examining a full Spark Ark.
“Glowing ball of energy not explode, right?”
–Wolfgang, when examining a full Spark Ark.
“It must be nice.”
–Wendy, when examining a full Spark Ark.
“GIVE ME YOUR POWER”
–WX-78, when examining a full Spark Ark.
“The glowing object inside emits some sort of energy.”
–Wickerbottom, when examining a full Spark Ark.
“Great Lakes, I think she caught something!”
–Woodie, when examining a full Spark Ark.
“It's full. Someone do something.”
–Maxwell, when examining a full Spark Ark.
“It burns bright like the swörd öf Surtr.”
–Wigfrid, when examining a full Spark Ark.
“Oooh it glows. Can we touch it?”
–Webber, when examining a full Spark Ark.
“Something is cooking.”
–Warly, when examining a full Spark Ark.
“Hello, pretty light ball”
–Wormwood, when examining a full Spark Ark.
“Sure it's bright, but what else does it do?”
–Winona, when examining a full Spark Ark.
“It absorbed the orb!”
–Wortox, when examining a full Spark Ark.
“Oooh, like glowy ball.”
–Wurt, when examining a full Spark Ark.
“I wonder if there is a glowing ball trapping badge.”
–Walter, when examining a full Spark Ark.
“Well, it's caught something. Now what?”
–Wanda, when examining a full Spark Ark.
The Spark Ark is an Item in Don't Starve Together, introduced in From Beyond. It resembles the head of an Ancient Sentrypede.
The first Spark Ark is given to the player by the Grainy Transmission after three lunar-mutated bosses are defeated:
Crystal Deerclops
Armored Bearger
Possessed Varg
Subsequent Spark Arks can be acquired by defeating only one of the bosses mentioned above; however, players must still cycle through the mutations. To receive a Spark Ark from a boss, it must not be a part of the current cycle; the cycle must be completed by having defeated both of the other ones. It is possible to earn a reward for defeating the same boss twice in a row if said boss was the final one of the three that was killed, as the progress is completely reset for each cycle.
For example, if the first Spark Ark is received after the defeat of Crystal Deerclops, the next Spark Ark can be obtained by defeating any one of the three bosses. If Crystal Deerclops is the first boss in the cycle thereafter, the player is rewarded with a Spark Ark. Afterward, one cannot be obtained from Crystal Deerclops again until after both Armored Bearger and Possessed Varg are defeated to conclude the cycle.
To be used in crafting, the Spark Ark must be filled with energy at the activated Ancient Archive. When placed on the floor, the Ark absorbs the Security Pulse from the Ancient Guard Post.
The full Spark Ark can be used to craft the Polar Bearger Bin, Ice Crystaleyezer, and Howlitzer at the Brightsmithy.

It resembles the head of an [[Ancient Sentrypede]]. The first Spark Ark is given to the player by the [[Grainy Transmission]] after three lunar-mutated bosses are defeated: *[[Crystal Deerclops]] *[[Armored Bearger]] *[[Possessed Varg]] Subsequent Spark Arks can be acquired by defeating only one of the bosses mentioned above; however, players must still cycle through the mutations. To receive a Spark Ark from a boss, it must not be a part of the current cycle; the cycle must be completed by having defeated both of the other ones. It is possible to earn a reward for defeating the same boss twice in a row if said boss was the final one of the three that was killed, as the progress is completely reset for each cycle. For example, if the first Spark Ark is received after the defeat of Crystal Deerclops, the next Spark Ark can be obtained by defeating any one of the three bosses. If Crystal Deerclops is the first boss in the cycle thereafter, the player is rewarded with a Spark Ark. Afterward, one cannot be obtained from Crystal Deerclops again until after both Armored Bearger and Possessed Varg are defeated to conclude the cycle. To be used in crafting, the Spark Ark must be filled with energy at the activated [[Ancient Archive]]. When placed on the floor, the Ark absorbs the [[Security Pulse]] from the [[Ancient Guard Post]]. The full Spark Ark can be used to craft the [[Polar Bearger Bin]], [[Ice Crystaleyezer]], and [[Howlitzer]] at the [[Brightsmithy]]. == {{pic|32|Notional Fabricator}} Crafting == {{Post-Boss Content|Celestial Scion No Rift}} The Spark Ark cannot be crafted until [[Celestial Scion]] is defeated, after which it can no longer be dropped by the [[Grainy Transmission]]. Instead, it can be crafted at the [[Notional Fabricator]] using 1 [[Nucleation Fluid]], 2 [[Thulecite]] and 1 [[Restrained Static]]. == {{pic|32|Icon Tools}} Usage == {{Recipe |tool = Brightsmithy |item1 = Spark Ark |item2 = Thick Fur |item3 = Pure Brilliance |count3 = 3 |item4 = Moon Rock |count4 = 5 |result = Polar Bearger Bin }} {{Recipe |tool = Brightsmithy |item1 = Spark Ark |item2 = Moon Shard |count2 = 8 |item3 = Pure Brilliance |count3 = 3 |item4 = Moon Rock |count4 = 5 |result= Ice Crystaleyezer Kit }} {{Recipe |tool = Brightsmithy |item1 = Spark Ark |item2 = Brightshade Husk |count2 = 3 |item3= Pure Brilliance |count3= 3 |item4 = Moon Rock |count4 = 3 |result= Howlitzer }} =={{Pic|32|Placeholder}} Trivia== * The Spark Ark was introduced in the ''[[From Beyond#Host of Horrors|Host of Horrors]]'' update. * On initial beta release, prior to Update 576123, it was only possible to acquire extra Spark Arks after defeating all three lunar-mutated bosses again. =={{Pic|32|Blueprint}} Gallery== Spark Ark Dropped.png|Spark Ark Dropped Spark Ark Activated Dropped.png|Spark Ark Dropped Spark Ark Idle animation.gif|Spark Ark Idle animation Spark Ark Idle animation2.gif|Spark Ark Idle animation Spark Ark trap.gif|Spark Ark Trap animation {{clear}} Resources= {{Resources}} |-| Craftable Items= {{Craftable Items}} [[Category:From Beyond]] [[vi:Spark Ark]] [[zh:火花柜]]
